tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 48 hours: Back to the start.

As the war rages on, Fujairah as the main tanker shuttle port is all but dead after the IRGC fired missiles at two VLCCs, the first escalation of its type yesterday.

Even with US protection, it was not enough, and this is going to take ~6 million b/d of flow out of the market.

To further add to my point that the escalation is increasing, this segment from the WSJ article is very concerning:

"There was also concern among shippers that the scope of Iran’s attacks was widening away from the immediate vicinity of the strait after a vessel was targeted off Oman early Tuesday, according to its owner, London-based Stolt-Nielsen. It had not crossed the strait at all and was loaded with Omani chemicals headed to Malaysia, according to Kpler."

We are in no-man's land now. Either Iran controls the Strait of Hormuz or it doesn't. It's all-or-nothing.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic.

As the war rages on, Fujairah as the main tanker shuttle port is all but dead after the IRGC fired missiles at two VLCCs, the first escalation of its type yesterday.

Even with US protection, it was not enough, and this is going to take ~6 million b/d of flow out of the market.

To further add to my point that the escalation is increasing, this segment from the WSJ article is very concerning:

"There was also concern among shippers that the scope of Iran’s attacks was widening away from the immediate vicinity of the strait after a vessel was targeted off Oman early Tuesday, according to its owner, London-based Stolt-Nielsen. It had not crossed the strait at all and was loaded with Omani chemicals headed to Malaysia, according to Kpler."

We are in no-man's land now. Either Iran controls the Strait of Hormuz or it doesn't. It's all-or-nothing.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic.

tweets.text:
Fujairah is done.

"Sinokor and MSC executives are concerned the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps will continue to attack their ships to try to shut down the shuttle market, said people familiar with the matter. At least two captains on their tankers have refused to go through the strait in recent days."

tweets.text: When asked what the Fed would do to specifically help Black workers, I wish Warsh would have replied candidly that Fed policy can't target individuals based on their race. Also, he should have pointed out that the biggest problems black workers face are laws passed by Congress.

tweets.text: Warsh said he prefers using interest rates over the Fed’s balance sheet for monetary policy, as the former doesn't favor one class of people over another. Of course they do. Artificially low interest rates favor wealthy, leveraged borrowers at the expense of middle-class savers.

tweets.text: The biggest problem with Warsh hearing is discussing inflation without a clear understanding of what the word means. If you incorrectly define inflation as rising prices instead of an expansion of the supply of money and credit, any discussion that follows becomes meaningless.
tweets.full_text: The biggest problem with Warsh hearing is discussing inflation without a clear understanding of what the word means. If you incorrectly define inflation as rising prices instead of an expansion of the supply of money and credit, any discussion that follows becomes meaningless.

tweets.text:
$BTC movement has been pretty clean from a technical perspective.

Standard sequence of breakdown, consolidation, bearish retest and back down since $106K

This recent low has shown a bit more strength, specifically in structure. No clean follow through on the breakdown is nice.

Needs more time to form a bottom and I wouldn't put on risk unless we close the daily above $67.2K

If we get a test of the weekly level around $54-55K, that's a good blind buy the first time.

I don't usually factor in too many headline news because:

1. A lot of them are just click-bait (noise)

2. The only ones that have consistently moved price and sentiment is Saylor and Trump-Iran news.

3. Hard to gauge influence to any of the headlines

General Perspective:

The goal is to make life-changing money. Most of you are not going to notice a difference in the trajectory of that goal by catching Bitcoin a few % higher.

Waiting for a new uptrend to form and then bidding choice ALTs is a more probable strategy to go from zero-to-hero.

However, that needs patience but since we lack that, there's the abundant amount of scam memes that can separate us from our money in the meantime.
tweets.full_text:
$BTC movement has been pretty clean from a technical perspective.

Standard sequence of breakdown, consolidation, bearish retest and back down since $106K

This recent low has shown a bit more strength, specifically in structure. No clean follow through on the breakdown is nice.

Needs more time to form a bottom and I wouldn't put on risk unless we close the daily above $67.2K

If we get a test of the weekly level around $54-55K, that's a good blind buy the first time.

I don't usually factor in too many headline news because:

1. A lot of them are just click-bait (noise)

2. The only ones that have consistently moved price and sentiment is Saylor and Trump-Iran news.

3. Hard to gauge influence to any of the headlines

General Perspective:

The goal is to make life-changing money. Most of you are not going to notice a difference in the trajectory of that goal by catching Bitcoin a few % higher.

Waiting for a new uptrend to form and then bidding choice ALTs is a more probable strategy to go from zero-to-hero.

However, that needs patience but since we lack that, there's the abundant amount of scam memes that can separate us from our money in the meantime.
